title: Integrating Acceptance and Commitment Therapy with Islāmic
book: Integrating Acceptance and Commitment Therapy with Islāmic

node_types.description: Stores demographic, cultural, religious, and psychosocial information about the client relevant to therapy and Islamic-cultural alignment.

properties.name: faith_identity_importance
properties.description: Importance of faith identity to client

properties.name: pain_history
properties.description: Summary of client's chronic or acute pain history

properties.name: psychosocial_context
properties.description: Relevant social, emotional, and economic context

node_types.description: Stores information about the therapist, including cultural competence and experience with Islamic-adapted therapies.

properties.name: cultural_competence
properties.description: Therapist's experience or training in Islamic-cultural adaptation

properties.name: languages_spoken
properties.description: Languages the therapist can use in therapy

properties.name: religious_background
properties.description: Therapist's religious or spiritual background (if relevant)

node_types.description: Represents a core Acceptance and Commitment Therapy process, including its definition and Islamic adaptation.

properties.name: process_name
properties.description: Name of the ACT process (e.g., acceptance, cognitive defusion)

properties.name: islamic_alignment
properties.description: Islamic concepts or practices aligned with this process

properties.name: therapeutic_exercises
properties.description: Exercises or metaphors used for this process

node_types.description: Stores an Islamic concept, value, or practice relevant to therapy (e.g., sabr, shukr, dhikr, tawakkul).

properties.name: practical_application
properties.description: How the concept is applied in therapy

node_types.description: Represents a specific therapeutic exercise, worksheet, or handout, including its Islamic adaptation.

properties.name: session_usage
properties.description: Which session(s) or processes use this exercise

properties.name: download_link
properties.description: Link to downloadable resource, if available

node_types.description: Represents a session or module in the ACT program, including session goals, structure, and Islamic-cultural adaptations.
Represents a barrier to effective therapy, including cultural, religious, language, or systemic factors.

properties.name: barrier_type
properties.description: Type of barrier (e.g., language, cultural misunderstanding)

properties.name: impact
properties.description: Impact on therapy or client outcomes

node_types.description: Represents a clinical or cultural risk factor relevant to therapy (e.g., suicide risk, medication side effects, negative religious coping).

properties.name: risk_type
properties.description: Type of risk (e.g., suicide, overexertion, negative coping)

properties.name: islamic_consideration
properties.description: Islamic perspective or guidance on this risk

properties.name: management_strategy
properties.description: How the risk is managed in therapy

node_types.description: Stores references to Islamic texts, figures, or stories used in therapy (e.g., Quran, Hadith, Prophet stories).

properties.name: reference_type
properties.description: Type of reference (Quran, Hadith, Story, Figure)

properties.name: reference_text
properties.description: Text or summary of the reference

properties.name: relevance
properties.description: How this reference is used in therapy

edge_types.description: Indicates that a client participates in a specific treatment session.
Indicates that a therapist delivers or facilitates a treatment session.
Indicates that a treatment session uses a specific therapeutic exercise or worksheet.
Indicates that an ACT process is aligned with a specific Islamic concept or value.
Indicates that a therapeutic exercise integrates a specific Islamic concept or practice.
Indicates that an Islamic concept is supported by a specific Quranic verse, Hadith, or story.
Indicates that a client faces a specific barrier to therapy.
Indicates that a client has a specific risk factor relevant to therapy.
Indicates that a treatment session addresses or adapts for a specific barrier to therapy.
Indicates that a treatment session manages or mitigates a specific risk factor.
